name: ETH Trend Reset Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: deeper pullback reset followed by renewed continuation
thesis: ETH 4h is the cleaner lane for testing whether a deeper reset into value can reload continuation better than shallow EMA-touch logic. The edge depends on resumed directional participation after a reset, not on blind trend-following in raw chop.
invalidation_condition: Abort if deeper resets still do not produce enough trades, if continuation only works on rare bars, or if chop overwhelms any post-reset persistence.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A prior directional leg resets deeper into value, stabilizes, and then resumes for a second push while risk remains defined against the reset zone.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition regimes often kill shallow pullback entries but still reward deeper resets that flush weak hands before the next leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is better than SOL 4h for this idea because continuation after reset is easier to express on a slightly cleaner, more liquid major.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the regime is too purely choppy, resets will not become continuations and the setup devolves into noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is acceptable but runner performance is poor, shorten the management structure before changing the reset definition.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_trade_density: Low-to-moderate; denser than shallow trend touch logic but less frequent than balance reversion.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower hit rate than the mean-reversion spec but larger average winners when reset continuation actually engages.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: At least one lane shows plausible density and continuation legs large enough to justify asymmetric exits after costs.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, repeated one-bar pops with no second leg, or only thin low-trade outputs.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: moderate but acceptable; reset and reacceleration are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: reduced by using deeper-value reset rather than exact swing-pattern choreography

test_lanes.why_included: Best clean lane for testing reset continuation in a transition-heavy environment.
test_lanes.continue_if: Continuation legs emerge after resets with enough trade count to clear screen credibly.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Reset entries trigger too rarely or never extend beyond short scalps.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: ETH should show a small set of cleaner second-leg continuations after deeper resets.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same mechanism strengthens on a higher-beta version of the lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: SOL shows similar logic with more amplitude but not catastrophic noise.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: Only SOL works and ETH does not, suggesting the mechanism is just volatility harvesting.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Bigger post-reset legs than ETH, but more variance around entries.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether reset continuation survives one timeframe faster without becoming ceremonial sparse logic.
test_lanes.continue_if: Signal density improves while continuation still produces structured winners.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: 1h becomes all noise or requires cosmetic threshold fiddling.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: More frequent resets and smaller but still tradable second-leg continuations.

long: Exit remaining 25% on close through EMA_55 or 5 bars
short: Exit remaining 25% on close through EMA_55 or 5 bars
